ex.en: Ninety people came to the concert.

no.en: used to give a negative answer to a question

ex.en: "Do you like coffee?" "No, I don't."
There is no milk in the fridge.

noise.en: a sound, especially one that is loud or unpleasant

ex.en: The children were making a lot of noise in the classroom.
ex.ko: 아이들이 교실에서 시끄러운 소리를 내고 있었다.

nose.en: the part of your face used for breathing and smelling

ex.en: She has a small nose.

not.en: used to make a word or sentence negative

ex.en: I am not hungry.

notebook.en: a book with blank or lined pages for writing notes

ex.en: She wrote the homework in her notebook.
He carries his notebook to class every day.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 노트북을 들고 수업에 간다.

ex.en: There is nothing in the box.

november.en: the eleventh month of the year

ex.en: Her birthday is in November.
I have to go now.

number.en: a word or symbol that represents an amount or quantity

ex.en: Choose a number between one and ten.

number.en: a series of digits used to identify something, such as a phone or a seat

ex.en: Can I have your phone number?

nurse.en: a person trained to take care of sick or injured people, especially in a hospital

ex.en: The nurse checked his temperature.

nurse.en: take care of a sick or injured person

ex.en: She nursed her father back to health.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아버지를 간호해 건강을 되찾게 했다.

o'clock.en: used after a number to say the exact time

ex.en: The train leaves at six o'clock.

october.en: the tenth month of the year

ex.en: School starts again in October.

of.en: used to show that something belongs to or is connected with something else

ex.en: This is the capital of France.

off.en: away from a place or position

ex.en: He took his coat off.

off.en: not turned on or not working

ex.en: Please make sure the lights are off.

office.en: a room or building where people work at desks

ex.en: She works in an office downtown.

officer.en: a member of the police force

ex.en: A police officer stopped the car.

officer.en: a person who holds a position of command in the armed forces

ex.en: The officer gave orders to the soldiers.
I often go for a walk after dinner.
ex.ko: 나는 저녁 식사 후에 자주 산책을 한다.

ok.en: used to show agreement or understanding

ex.en: "Let's go now." "OK, let's go."
Is everything OK?

old.en: having lived for a long time

ex.en: My grandfather is very old.

old.en: having existed for a long time; not new

ex.en: This is an old building from the 1800s.

olympics.en: a major international sports event held every four years

ex.en: She won a gold medal at the Olympics.

on.en: positioned on top of or touching a surface

ex.en: The book is on the table.

on.en: used before a day or date to say when something happens

ex.en: We have a meeting on Monday.
Please turn the light on.

once.en: one time only, or at some time in the past

ex.en: I have been to Paris once.
ex.ko: 나는 파리에 한 번 가본 적이 있다.

ex.en: Once you finish your homework, you can watch TV.
ex.ko: 일단 숙제를 끝내면 텔레비전을 볼 수 있다.

ex.en: I only need one ticket.

only.en: and nothing or no one else

ex.en: I only have five dollars.

only.en: being the single one that exists

ex.en: She is the only child in her family.

open.en: move something so it is no longer closed

ex.en: Can you open the window?
The door was open when I arrived.
ex.ko: 내가 도착했을 때 문이 열려 있었다.

ex.en: The store is open until nine tonight.

opera.en: a play in which the words are sung to music

ex.en: They went to see an opera last night.
Would you like tea or coffee?

orange.en: a round citrus fruit with a thick orange skin

ex.en: She squeezed fresh orange juice.

orange.en: a color between red and yellow

ex.en: He painted the fence orange.

other.en: different from the one already mentioned

ex.en: I have two dogs; the other one is a puppy.
ex.ko: 나는 개가 두 마리 있는데, 다른 한 마리는 강아지다.

ex.en: This is our house.

ours.en: the thing or things belonging to us

ex.en: That car is ours.

out.en: away from the inside of a place

ex.en: She went out to buy some milk.

out.en: not at home or not in one's usual place

ex.en: I'm sorry, he's out right now.
By midnight, all the lights were out.

outside.en: not inside a building; in the open air

ex.en: The children are playing outside.

outside.en: the outer part or surface of something

ex.en: The outside of the box was blue.
The plane flew over the mountains.
The movie is over now.

own.en: belonging to oneself, not shared or borrowed

ex.en: She has her own room.
They own a small house near the beach.
ex.ko: 그들은 해변 근처에 작은 집을 소유하고 있다.

ex.en: He is the owner of that restaurant.

page.en: one side of a sheet of paper in a book

ex.en: Please turn to page ten.

paint.en: a colored liquid used to cover a surface

ex.en: We bought some white paint for the fence.
ex.ko: 우리는 울타리에 칠할 흰색 페인트를 샀다.

paint.en: cover a surface with paint, or create a picture using paint

ex.en: She painted the wall blue.
There is a beautiful painting on the wall.

pair.en: two things of the same kind that go together

ex.en: I bought a new pair of shoes.
ex.ko: 나는 새 신발 한 켤레를 샀다.

palace.en: a large, grand house where a king, queen, or other important person lives

ex.en: The king lived in a huge palace.

pants.en: a piece of clothing that covers the legs separately

ex.en: He was wearing black pants.

paper.en: thin material used for writing or wrapping

ex.en: She wrote her name on a piece of paper.
ex.ko: 그녀는 종이 한 장에 자기 이름을 적었다.

ex.en: My father reads the paper every morning.
ex.ko: 우리 아버지는 매일 아침 신문을 읽으신다.

paragraph.en: a section of a piece of writing that starts on a new line

ex.en: Write a short paragraph about your family.
ex.ko: 가족에 관해 짧은 단락을 써 보세요.

ex.en: Please pardon my mistake.

pardon.en: used to politely ask someone to repeat what they said

ex.en: Pardon? Could you say that again?
Both of my parents work in the city.
ex.ko: 우리 부모님 두 분 다 도시에서 일하신다.

partner.en: a person who works with you or does an activity together with you

ex.en: Choose a partner for the dance.

partner.en: the person someone is married to or in a romantic relationship with

ex.en: She introduced me to her partner.

party.en: a social event where people gather to enjoy themselves

ex.en: We're having a birthday party on Saturday.
ex.ko: 우리는 토요일에 생일 파티를 열 것이다.

party.en: an organized group of people with the same political ideas

ex.en: He joined a political party after college.
ex.ko: 그는 대학 졸업 후 정당에 가입했다.

past.en: belonging to a time before now

ex.en: In the past year, she has learned a lot.
ex.ko: 지난 1년 동안 그녀는 많은 것을 배웠다.

ex.en: We should learn from the past.

past.en: beyond a particular time or place

ex.en: It's ten past six.
I paid for the tickets online.

pay.en: money you earn from a job

ex.en: He asked for a pay raise.

peace.en: a state without war or conflict

ex.en: The two countries finally made peace.

pen.en: a tool used for writing with ink

ex.en: Can I borrow your pen?

pencil.en: a thin wooden tool used for writing or drawing

ex.en: She wrote the answer in pencil.
Many people were waiting at the station.
She lived in Paris for a period of three years.

period.en: the punctuation mark (.) used at the end of a sentence

ex.en: Don't forget to put a period at the end of the sentence.
ex.ko: 문장 끝에 마침표 찍는 것을 잊지 마.

ex.en: She is a kind person.

personal.en: relating to a particular person, not to others

ex.en: That's a personal question.

pet.en: an animal that people keep at home for companionship

ex.en: Do you have any pets?

phone.en: a device used to talk to someone who is far away

ex.en: My phone is out of battery.
I'll phone you tonight.

photo.en: a picture taken with a camera

ex.en: He took a photo of the sunset.

piano.en: a large musical instrument played by pressing black and white keys

ex.en: She practices piano every afternoon.
Pick a card, any card.

pick.en: pull a flower or fruit off a plant

ex.en: We picked apples at the farm.

picnic.en: an outdoor meal or trip where people eat food outside

ex.en: We went on a picnic by the lake.

picture.en: a drawing, painting, or photograph of something

ex.en: She drew a picture of her house.

picture.en: form a picture of something in your mind

ex.en: Try to picture yourself on a beach.
ex.ko: 해변에 있는 너 자신을 상상해 봐.

piece.en: a part of something that has been separated from a whole

ex.en: Would you like a piece of cake?

piece.en: a single work of music, art, or writing

ex.en: The pianist played a beautiful piece.

pig.en: a farm animal with a curly tail, often raised for meat

ex.en: The pig was rolling in the mud.
She wore a pink dress to the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에 분홍색 드레스를 입고 갔다.

pizza.en: a round, flat Italian dish topped with cheese and tomato sauce

ex.en: Let's order a pizza for dinner.

place.en: a particular position, area, or building

ex.en: This is a nice place to relax.

place.en: put something in a particular position

ex.en: She placed the book on the shelf.

plan.en: decide in detail what you will do in the future

ex.en: We are planning a trip to Japan.

plan.en: a set of decisions about how to do something in the future

ex.en: Do you have any plans for the weekend?

plane.en: a vehicle with wings that flies through the air

ex.en: We took a plane to New York.

player.en: a person who takes part in a sport or game

ex.en: He is the best player on the team.

player.en: a person who plays a musical instrument

ex.en: She is a talented piano player.

please.en: used to make a request more polite

ex.en: Could you help me, please?

please.en: make someone feel happy or satisfied

ex.en: She always tries to please her parents.
ex.ko: 그녀는 항상 부모님을 기쁘게 해드리려 노력한다.

pleasure.en: a feeling of happiness or satisfaction

ex.en: Reading gives her great pleasure.

pocket.en: a small pouch sewn into clothing for carrying things

ex.en: He put his keys in his pocket.

poem.en: a piece of writing arranged in lines, often with rhythm or rhyme

ex.en: She wrote a poem about the ocean.

point.en: show where something is by extending your finger toward it

ex.en: She pointed at the map.

point.en: a unit used for scoring in a game or test

ex.en: Our team scored ten points.

point.en: the main idea or purpose of what someone is saying

ex.en: What's your point?

pollution.en: the presence of harmful substances that damage the environment

ex.en: Air pollution is a serious problem in big cities.

pool.en: a large container filled with water for swimming

ex.en: The kids are swimming in the pool.

pool.en: a small area of still water

ex.en: There was a pool of water after the rain.
They were too poor to buy new clothes.
ex.ko: 그들은 너무 가난해서 새 옷을 살 수 없었다.

ex.en: The poor dog was left in the rain.
ex.ko: 그 불쌍한 개는 비를 맞으며 남겨졌다.

pop.en: appear or move suddenly and quickly

ex.en: He popped out from behind the door.

pop.en: popular music enjoyed by many people, especially young people

ex.en: She loves listening to pop music.
ex.ko: 그녀는 팝 음악 듣는 것을 좋아한다.

post.en: put a message or information where others can see it, especially online

ex.en: She posted a photo on social media.

post.en: the system for sending letters and packages

ex.en: I sent the letter by post.

poster.en: a large printed picture or notice put up on a wall

ex.en: There's a movie poster on his bedroom wall.
ex.ko: 그의 방 벽에는 영화 포스터가 붙어 있다.

potato.en: a round vegetable that grows underground

ex.en: She peeled the potatoes for dinner.
ex.ko: 그녀는 저녁 식사를 위해 감자 껍질을 벗겼다.

practice.en: the activity of repeating something to get better at it

ex.en: Piano practice takes an hour every day.
ex.ko: 피아노 연습은 매일 한 시간이 걸린다.

practice.en: do something repeatedly to improve a skill

ex.en: He practices soccer every afternoon.

pray.en: speak to God or a god, especially to give thanks or ask for something

ex.en: They prayed for peace.

pretty.en: pleasant to look at, especially attractive in a delicate way

ex.en: She has a pretty smile.
The test was pretty difficult.

price.en: the amount of money you need to pay for something

ex.en: What's the price of this jacket?

price.en: decide how much something will cost

ex.en: The shop priced the shoes at fifty dollars.

prince.en: the son of a king or queen

ex.en: The prince will become king one day.
ex.ko: 그 왕자는 언젠가 왕이 될 것이다.

princess.en: the daughter of a king or queen

ex.en: The princess lived in a castle.

problem.en: a difficult situation that needs to be solved

ex.en: We have a big problem to solve.
ex.ko: 우리는 해결해야 할 큰 문제가 있다.

program.en: a planned series of events, or a show on TV or radio

ex.en: What's your favorite TV program?
ex.ko: 네가 가장 좋아하는 TV 프로그램은 뭐야?

program.en: write instructions for a computer to follow

ex.en: He programs computer games for fun.

purple.en: a color between red and blue

ex.en: She wore a purple scarf.

push.en: use force to move something away from you

ex.en: Push the door to open it.

put.en: move something to a particular place or position

ex.en: Put the box on the table.

quarter.en: one of four equal parts of something

ex.en: Cut the apple into quarters.

quarter.en: a US or Canadian coin worth 25 cents

ex.en: I need a quarter for the parking meter.

question.en: something you ask in order to get information

ex.en: Can I ask you a question?

question.en: express doubt about whether something is true or correct

ex.en: Nobody questioned his honesty.
ex.ko: 아무도 그의 정직함에 의문을 제기하지 않았다.

ex.en: She ran quickly to catch the bus.
Please be quiet in the library.

quiet.en: a state of silence or calm

ex.en: I enjoy the quiet of the early morning.

rabbit.en: a small animal with long ears that lives in a burrow

ex.en: The rabbit hopped across the garden.

radio.en: a device that receives broadcast sound programs

ex.en: He listens to the radio while driving.

rain.en: water that falls from the sky in drops

ex.en: We got wet in the rain.
It's raining outside.
It was a rainy day, so we stayed inside.
ex.ko: 비 오는 날이라서 우리는 안에 있었다.

rat.en: a small animal with a long tail, larger than a mouse

ex.en: A rat ran across the kitchen floor.
ex.ko: 쥐 한 마리가 부엌 바닥을 가로질러 뛰어갔다.

read.en: look at written words and understand their meaning

ex.en: She reads a book every night.

reader.en: a person who reads, especially a particular book or publication

ex.en: This magazine has many young readers.

reading.en: the activity of reading books or other written material

ex.en: Reading is her favorite hobby.

ready.en: prepared and able to do something

ex.en: Are you ready to go?

real.en: actually existing, not imagined or false

ex.en: Is that a real diamond?

really.en: used to emphasize that something is true

ex.en: I really like this song.

reason.en: the cause or explanation for something

ex.en: What is the reason for the delay?

red.en: the color of blood or fire

ex.en: She was wearing a red dress.

remember.en: keep something in your mind or recall it

ex.en: I can't remember his name.
Could you repeat that, please?

reporter.en: a person who writes or presents news stories

ex.en: A reporter asked the mayor a question.

restaurant.en: a place where you can buy and eat a meal

ex.en: We had dinner at a nice restaurant.

result.cue: 어떤 일이 일어난 뒤에 생기는 것
result.en: something that happens because of something else

ex.en: What was the result of the exam?
The accident resulted in serious damage.

review.en: look at or study something again to check or improve it

ex.en: She reviewed her notes before the test.

review.en: a written opinion about the quality of something, such as a book or movie

ex.en: The movie got great reviews.

ribbon.en: a narrow strip of decorative material used for tying or decorating

ex.en: She tied her hair with a red ribbon.

rice.en: small white or brown grains that are cooked and eaten as food

ex.en: We eat rice almost every day.
He became rich after starting his own business.

rich.en: having a large amount of something good or valuable

ex.en: This soil is rich in nutrients.

ride.en: sit on and control a bicycle, horse, or other vehicle to travel

ex.en: He rides his bike to school.

ride.en: a trip made in or on a vehicle

ex.en: Can I have a ride to the station?

river.en: a large natural stream of water that flows into the sea

ex.en: They swam in the river.

role.en: the function or part someone has in a situation or group

ex.en: She plays an important role in the company.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회사에서 중요한 역할을 맡고 있다.

room.en: a separate space inside a building, enclosed by walls

ex.en: My room is upstairs.

room.en: share a room or live in a place with someone

ex.en: I roomed with my cousin during college.
ex.ko: 나는 대학 시절 사촌과 방을 같이 썼다.

rose.en: a flower with a sweet smell that grows on a bush with thorns

ex.en: He gave her a bouquet of red roses.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀에게 빨간 장미 꽃다발을 주었다.

row.en: a line of people or things placed next to each other

ex.en: We sat in the front row.

row.en: move a boat through water using oars

ex.en: They rowed across the lake.
It was rude of him to interrupt.

rule.en: an instruction that says what you must or must not do

ex.en: You have to follow the rules of the game.

rule.en: have official power over a country or area

ex.en: The king ruled the country for forty years.

ruler.en: a flat tool used for measuring or drawing straight lines

ex.en: Use a ruler to draw a straight line.

ruler.en: a person who governs a country

ex.en: The ruler made a new law.

sad.en: feeling unhappy, especially because something bad has happened

ex.en: She felt sad when her dog died.

sailor.en: a person who works on a ship

ex.en: The sailor climbed up the mast.

salad.en: a cold dish made of mixed vegetables

ex.en: She ordered a salad for lunch.

sale.cue: 물건을 파는 일 또는 할인 판매
sale.en: the act of selling something, or an event where prices are reduced

ex.en: The store is having a big sale this weekend.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 이번 주말에 큰 세일을 한다.

ex.en: We wore the same shirt by accident.

sandwich.en: food made of two slices of bread with filling in between

ex.en: He made a ham sandwich for lunch.

saturday.en: the day of the week after Friday and before Sunday

ex.en: We're going to the park on Saturday.

saw.en: a tool with a sharp blade used for cutting wood

ex.en: He cut the branch with a saw.
She sawed the log into pieces.
She said she was tired.

say.en: the right or chance to express your opinion

ex.en: Everyone should have a say in the decision.
ex.ko: 모든 사람이 그 결정에 발언권을 가져야 한다.

school.en: a place where children go to learn

ex.en: He walks to school every morning.

science.en: the study of the natural world through observation and experiments

ex.en: She loves science class.

scientist.en: a person who studies or works in science

ex.en: The scientist discovered a new species.

sea.en: the large body of salt water that covers much of the earth

ex.en: They swam in the sea.

seat.en: a place where you can sit

ex.en: Please take a seat.

second.en: coming after the first in order

ex.en: She finished in second place.

second.en: a unit of time equal to one sixtieth of a minute

ex.en: Wait a few seconds, please.

section.en: one of the parts that something is divided into

ex.en: This section of the store sells shoes.
ex.ko: 이 가게의 이 구역에서는 신발을 판다.

see.en: notice or become aware of something using your eyes

ex.en: I can see the mountains from here.
Oh, I see what you mean.

self.en: a person's own individual identity or nature

ex.en: She wants to find her true self.

sell.en: give something to someone in exchange for money

ex.en: They sell fresh fruit at the market.
